title: ESG Currents: How Japan’s Governance Code Reshapes the Market
summary: Japan’s Corporate Governance Code, first introduced in 2015, has driven significant changes across the market – strengthening shareholder value, enhancing investor engagement, protecting minority rights, promoting gender diversity, and supporting the transition to a decarbonized economy. A further revision expected in 2026 could accelerate progress across these areas.

title: Odd Lots: Martin Wolf on the Roots of Our Recent Chaos (Podcast)
summary: Last year, when we talked to Martin Wolf, the global order seemed like it was being upended after President Trump unveiled his sweeping tariffs against nearly every US trading partner. A lot has happened since then. In fact, April 2025 seems almost quaint when compared to 2026 so far, from the Supreme Court’s tariff ruling to the US-Israel war with Iran. title: Two India-Bound LPG Tankers Add to Uptick in Hormuz Transits
summary: Two India-bound vessels laden with cooking fuel from the Persian Gulf appear to have transited the Strait of Hormuz, making them the latest to exit despite continued restrictions from the US and Iran.

title: Turkey Lifts Year-End Inflation Target to 24%, Citing Iran War
summary: Turkey’s central bank revised its year-end inflation target, citing the effects of higher energy prices resulting from the US-Israeli war on Iran.

title: 3i Slumps on Warning of Middle East Hit on Retailer Action
summary: 3i Group Plc slumped after the private equity firm flagged slowing sales at discount retailer Action — which represents more than two thirds of its portfolio value — in a sign that even low-cost stores are struggling as inflation squeezes consumers’ wallets.

title: Chinese Stocks Fall as Markets Await Signals From Xi-Trump Talks
summary: Chinese equities slipped as President Xi Jinping met with his US counterpart Donald Trump, with investors taking some profits after a recent tech-driven rally while waiting for clearer signals from the talks.

summary: The relentless rally in technology stocks is showing little sign of cooling as a blockbuster share sale and Cisco Systems Inc.’s earnings highlighted the momentum around artificial intelligence.

title: China Fuel Demand Under Pressure as Pricier Oil Adds to EV Push
summary: China’s gasoline demand is expected to slide further this year, as the Iran war pushes up oil prices and accelerates a long-term shift away from the internal combustion engine.

title: War Turmoil Saps Asian Reserves With Philippines, India Hit Most
summary: Foreign-exchange reserves are slumping across Asia as policymakers shell out funds to defend their currencies against the spike in oil prices caused by the Iran war.

title: China Renews Import Permits for US Beef Plants as Xi, Trump Meet
summary: China has renewed import licenses for hundreds of US beef plants, reviving trade in the meat as the leaders of the world’s two biggest economies meet in Beijing to stabilize commercial and geopolitical relations.

title: Watches of Switzerland Hits Record Driven by US Watch Collectors
summary: Watches of Switzerland Group Plc revenues surged to a record last year and profit will come in higher than expected after strong sales in the vital US market.

summary: A flurry of block trades is indicating that India’s equity capital markets may be in the early stages of a revival after a subdued year so far.

title: Historic Chemicals Downturn Upends a PE Firm’s Big Bet on Sector
summary: A $10 billion private equity firm that made its name as a chemicals specialist has seen a wave of its holdings fall into distress amid a severe industry downturn, pushing the investor to adapt its strategy.

title: India Considers Reducing Taxes on Bond Investments by Foreigners
summary: India is considering a significant reduction in the taxes paid by foreign investors on the nation’s bonds as authorities seek to align policies with global norms and attract inflows, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Wyne: Trump has Little Interest in Taiwan Security
summary:
Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping signalled a relationship reset as the two leaders set a positive tone at their historic summit in Beijing. Meanwhile President Xi warned that Taiwan remains the most critical issue in China-US relations. Ali Wyne, Senior Research and Advocacy Advisor for U.S.-China Relations at the International Crisis Group spoke to Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the visit between the two leaders.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Bond Binge Overwhelms Wall Street, Pushes Alphabet Abroad
summary: Bankers were still putting the final touches on Alphabet Inc.’s blockbuster $17 billion of bond sales when word started to spread Monday morning on Wall Street: the company is already hawking more debt.

title: Australia Steel-Making Coal Recovery Stalls, Data Firm Says
summary: A number of Australian steel-making coal mines may have struggled to sustain recent recoveries in output, potentially supporting prices of the commodity, according to a satellite-data analytics firm.

title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai Profit Jumps After AI Fuels Server Sales
summary: Nvidia Corp.’s major server assembly partner Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a stronger-than-expected increase in quarterly profit, highlighting sustained spending on hardware essential for AI.

title: Japan’s Long-Dated Bonds Slump as Global Rout Adds Pressure
summary: Longer-dated Japanese government bonds fell on Thursday, even after solid demand at a 30-year auction, as rising global yields continued to weigh on the market.

title: India Bans Sugar Exports for Four Months to Protect Supply
summary: India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, has banned exports for more than four months, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies.

title: Gold Holds Steady as Climbing US Inflation Raises Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold was steady as a resurgence in US inflation reinforced bets the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: Manipal Hospitals Said to Start Marketing $1 Billion IPO Soon
summary: Manipal Health Enterprises Pvt., which runs the Manipal Hospitals chain, is set to begin formal marketing as early as next week for its planned initial public offering to raise more than $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Japanese Crude Tanker Emerges Outside Hormuz in Rare Transit
summary: A Japanese supertanker has emerged in the Gulf of Oman after last signaling that it was inside the Persian Gulf, indicating a rare, undercover transit through the Strait of Hormuz by a vessel from the Asian country.

title: Trump, Xi Meet With Iran and Trade in Focus | The Asia Trade 5/14/2026

(Source: Bloomberg)

title: Abu Dhabi’s L’imad Eyes $30 Billion Infra Push With Temasek, GIP
summary: Abu Dhabi’s L’imad Holding is joining local and global partners in an investment venture targeting $30 billion of infrastructure projects, underscoring the central role the new sovereign wealth fund is likely to play in the emirate’s ambitions.
